channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Why Identity Security Is Your Cyber Career Entry Point
item.description: As AI reshapes cybersecurity workflows, John Paul Cunningham, CISO at SIlverfort, says the technology is creating opportunities rather than eliminating jobs — and there are more ways than ever to break into the essential field.
item.dc:creator: Kristina Beek

item.title: Phishers Gain Persistence at EU, Asia Hospitality Orgs
item.description: Separate but similar campaigns described by Microsoft and Trend Micro use malicious zip files to spread malware via social engineering and obsfucation, including blockchain abuse.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Microsoft Warns Poisoned MCP Tool Descriptions Can Make AI Agents Leak Data
item.description:
New Microsoft&nbsp;research&nbsp;shows how attackers can hijack AI agents that act on a user's behalf, using nothing more than a poisoned tool description to make the agent quietly hand over company data to an outsider.
The trick is that the agent never breaks a rule. Every step looks routine, so in a default setup no alarm may fire.
The work comes from Microsoft Incident Response and its
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: RustDuck Botnet Rebuilds in Rust to Hijack Routers and Servers for DDoS
item.description:
A new two-stage malware family called RustDuck is hijacking home routers, IP cameras, Android boxes, and poorly secured servers, then stitching them into a network built to knock websites and online services offline.
Researchers at QiAnXin's&nbsp;XLab&nbsp;have tracked it since February 2026, and say the real story is not how big it is today, but how fast it is changing.
The end goal is a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Nissan Discloses Employee Data Breach Linked to Oracle Zero-Day
item.description: Nissan says employees' data was stolen via the Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day campaign

item.title: Bring PCI SSC Training to Your Organization with the New Training Venue Host Program
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Langflow RCE Exploited to Deploy Monero Miner on Exposed AI App Endpoints
item.description:
Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical Langflow vulnerability as part of fresh attacks designed to deliver a Monero cryptocurrency miner.
The activity has been found to weaponize CVE-2026-33017 (CVSS score: 9.3), an unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Langflow, indicating threat actors are scanning and targeting exposed artificial intelligence (AI)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Silent Swap Crypto Clipper Uses Fake Google Notes Extension to Replace Wallet Addresses
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged an active browser extension campaign that is designed to steal cryptocurrency by stealthily replacing wallet addresses when unsuspecting users initiate a transaction.
The cryptocurrency clipper activity has been codenamed Silent Swap by McAfee Labs.
"The campaign is delivered through unsigned installers – observed in both .NET and Golang variants – that
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Critical SimpleHelp Vulnerability Exploited For Malware Delivery
item.description: Attackers exploited a critical SimpleHelp RMM bug to deploy TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer malware

item.title: GuardFall Exposes Open-Source AI Coding Agents to Decades-Old Shell Injection Risks
item.description:
The safety check that is supposed to stop an AI coding agent from running a dangerous command can be walked straight past using a shell trick that has been public for decades.
New research from&nbsp;Adversa AI, which is named the bypass GuardFall, found it works against ten of the eleven popular open-source coding and computer-use agents the firm tested. Only one, "Continue," was built to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: SimpleHelp Flaw Exploited to Deploy Malware Targeting Windows, macOS, and Linux
item.description: <p>A SimpleHelp authentication flaw is being exploited to deploy Djinn Stealer, a cross-platform malware targeting cloud, developer, and AI credentials.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-simplehelp-flaw-djinn-stealer-developer-credentials/">SimpleHelp Flaw Exploited to Deploy Malware Targeting Windows, macOS, and Linux</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: iPhone Security Fixes May Arrive Sooner as AI Speeds Up Threats
item.description: <p>Apple is releasing some iPhone security fixes earlier as AI raises concerns about faster cyberattacks and shorter patch windows. </p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-iphone-ai-security-updates/">iPhone Security Fixes May Arrive Sooner as AI Speeds Up Threats</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: BlueHammer Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: <p>The Microsoft Defender vulnerability CVE-2026-33825 was exploited in the wild as a zero-day before patches were released.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/bluehammer-vulnerability-exploited-in-ransomware-attacks/">BlueHammer Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: 282 iOS AI Apps Leak API Keys and Open AI Proxy Access in Network Traffic Study
item.description:
Researchers tested 444 AI chatbot apps for iPhone and found that 282 of them, nearly two-thirds, exposed paid AI access through their network traffic.
In many cases, the path in was visible just by watching what the app sent: a plaintext API key, a reusable token, or a backend server that accepted requests with no key at all.
Whoever grabs it can send model requests on the developer's account,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: iPhone 18 Leak: Apple’s Next Pro Design May Have Appeared Online
item.description: <p>Leaked Tata files reportedly show possible iPhone 18 Pro design details, factory images, and supplier records ahead of Apple’s expected September launch.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-iphone-18-pro-design-leak-tata-breach/">iPhone 18 Leak: Apple’s Next Pro Design May Have Appeared Online</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: AI-Generated Workflows Are a Silent Security Disaster
item.description: Teams are dealing with a truly dangerous problem — automation that works, but that no one understands.
item.dc:creator: Yelena Mujibur Sheikh

item.title: Decades-Old Bash Tricks Expose AI Coding Agents to Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: <p>Decades-old Bash shell tricks can bypass safeguards in most open source AI coding agents, potentially turning malicious repositories into supply chain attack vectors.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/decades-old-bash-tricks-expose-ai-coding-agents-to-supply-chain-attacks/">Decades-Old Bash Tricks Expose AI Coding Agents to Supply Chain Attacks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend

item.title: Aflac Japan Data Breach Impacts 4.38 Million
item.description: <p>Hackers accessed the insurance giant’s policyholder portal multiple times between June 15 and June 25.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/aflac-japan-data-breach-impacts-4-38-million/">Aflac Japan Data Breach Impacts 4.38 Million</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: The Realities of AI Video Surveillance
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: ClickFix Now Cybercriminals' Favorite Malware Delivery Technique
item.description: ReliaQuest report warns of a surge in ClickFix social engineering attacks against Windows and macOS users

item.title: Verifiable Digital Credential Presentment
item.description: This blog post is #4 in our series on Verifiable Digital Credentials (VDCs). Our other posts can be found via Post #1, Post #2, and Post #3. In earlier posts, we discussed how verifiable digital credentials (VDCs) are issued and compared the underlying credential formats (ISO/IEC “mdoc” vs. W3C Verifiable Credentials). In this post, we turn to the other side of the story: presentation; that is, how a holder shows their VDC to a verifier at runtime in both in-person and online contexts. We’ll again explore the mobile driver’s license (mDL) use case, consider the differences between ISO/IEC
item.dc:creator: Bill Fisher, Ryan Galluzzo, Heather Flanagan

item.title: Hacker Conversations: Chris Thompson, Former Head of IBM X-Force Red, Co-Founder of RemoteThreat
item.description: <p>Chris Thompson's journey took him from hacking game controls as a teenager to founding IBM’s X-Force Red team.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/hacker-conversations-chris-thompson-former-head-of-ibm-x-force-red-co-founder-of-remotethreat/">Hacker Conversations: Chris Thompson, Former Head of IBM X-Force Red, Co-Founder of RemoteThreat</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend

item.title: Supreme Court Rules Constitutional Privacy Protections Apply to Cellphone Users’ Location History
item.description: <p>The ruling was made in the case of a bank robber whose identity was discovered through a geofence warrant.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/supreme-court-rules-constitutional-privacy-protections-apply-to-cellphone-users-location-history/">Supreme Court Rules Constitutional Privacy Protections Apply to Cellphone Users&#8217; Location History</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Associated Press

item.title: What the Numbers Say About FIFA 2026 Cyber Risk
item.description:
The FIFA World Cup 2026 opened on June 11. By that date, according to Check Point Research, the fraud infrastructure targeting it had already been built, staged, and partially deployed. Threat actor activity was pre-planned, months out, across three sectors and at least ten languages.
Check Point Exposure Management published the FIFA World Cup 2026 Cyber Threat Report this month, covering
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Exploitation of Recent Oracle E-Business Suite Vulnerability Begins
item.description: <p>The critical-severity defect allows unauthenticated attackers to take over the E-Business Suite’s Payments product.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/exploitation-of-recent-oracle-e-business-suite-vulnerability-begins/">Exploitation of Recent Oracle E-Business Suite Vulnerability Begins</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Attackers Exploit SimpleHelp CVE-2026-48558 to Deploy TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer
item.description:
An unknown threat actor has been observed exploiting a recently disclosed maximum-severity security flaw in SimpleHelp to deliver two previously unreported malware families, TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer.
The intrusion involves the exploitation of CVE-2026-48558 (CVSS score: 10.0), a critical authentication bypass vulnerability impacting the OpenID Connect (OIDC) flow that an unauthenticated
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hackers Leverage Blockchain to Hit Japan's Hotels Through Booking.com Phishing
item.description: A wave of phishing emails sent to Booking.com partner accommodations in Japan in May led to blockchain-hosted malware

item.title: The AI Token Costs That Can Break Cybersecurity
item.description: <p>As cybersecurity platforms embrace agentic AI, organizations must balance detection performance against the escalating costs of token consumption, deployment architecture, and AI credits.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/the-ai-token-costs-that-can-break-cybersecurity/">The AI Token Costs That Can Break Cybersecurity</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Danelle Au

item.title: UK Healthcare Sector Records Tenfold Increase in Cyber-Attacks
item.description: SonicWall records 264,000 events in first five months of 2026 as UK hospitals come under siege

item.title: AirDrop and Quick Share Flaws Let Nearby Attackers Trigger Crashes and Bypass Checks
item.description:
Two researchers have found six security flaws in AirDrop and Quick Share, the wireless features that beam files between nearby devices with no cables or shared network.
An attacker within wireless range, with just a laptop and no prior connection, can crash the sharing service on a Mac or iPhone set to receive from anyone, with no tap or prompt.
The same research found Quick Share flaws that
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Nissan Employee Data Breached in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack
item.description: <p>Only a handful of the 100 organizations targeted in the PeopleSoft campaign have been confirmed.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/nissan-employee-data-breached-in-oracle-peoplesoft-hack/">Nissan Employee Data Breached in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Best Data Breach & Data Leak Detection Tools | UpGuard
item.description: Stopping an intrusion is half the battle. This guide ranks the top breach and leak detection tools to neutralize exposed credentials before attackers do.

item.title: Critical SimpleHelp Vulnerability Exploited for Malware Delivery
item.description: <p>The threat actor is focused on collecting credentials, SSH keys, cryptocurrency wallets, and development tooling.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/critical-simplehelp-vulnerability-exploited-for-malware-delivery/">Critical SimpleHelp Vulnerability Exploited for Malware Delivery</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: New BioShocking Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Leaking User Credentials
item.description:
Convince an AI browser that it is playing a game, and it can hand over your login details. That is the finding behind&nbsp;BioShocking, a technique from security firm LayerX that tricked six AI browsers and assistants into copying a user's credentials and sending them to an attacker.
The targets included OpenAI's ChatGPT Atlas, Perplexity's Comet, and Anthropic's Claude browser extension.
An
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Over 300 UK Firms Hit by Ransomware in a Year
item.description: Report Fraud data reveals that more than half of 323 UK ransomware victims last year were SMEs

item.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Flaw Could Let Attackers Run Root Commands Pre-Auth
item.description:
A critical vulnerability in Progress Kemp LoadMaster can let an unauthenticated attacker execute arbitrary commands as root on the appliance by sending a crafted request to its API.
The flaw, tracked as&nbsp;CVE-2026-8037, carries a CVSS score of&nbsp;9.8 according to ZDI. A patch is available. If you run LoadMaster with the API enabled, update now.
Progress&nbsp;published its advisory on June
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Quantifind Raises $200 Million for AI-Native Risk Intelligence
item.description: <p>Quantifind will accelerate international expansion and extend its platform’s localized risk intelligence capabilities.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/quantifind-raises-200-million-for-ai-native-risk-intelligence/">Quantifind Raises $200 Million for AI-Native Risk Intelligence</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited in the Wild
item.description:
A critical security flaw impacting Oracle E-Business Suite has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to Defused Cyber.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46817 (CVSS score: 9.8), refers to an improper privilege management and authentication flaw in Oracle Payments that could be abused to take over susceptible instances.
"Easily exploitable vulnerability allows
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What Is Sales Enablement? A Guide for Security and Compliance Teams | UpGuard
item.description: Discover why security reviews are the hidden sales enablement bottleneck, and learn a strategy for overcoming this hurdle to accelerate deal velocity.

item.title: Best Vulnerability Management Tools and Software in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: Compare the top vulnerability management software (VMS) platforms of 2026. This comprehensive guide reviews industry leaders like UpGuard, Tenable, Qualys,

item.title: Top 25 Cyberattacks in Sports: Does Defense Win Championships? | UpGuard
item.description: From malicious insider espionage to devastating double-extortion campaigns, here are the top 25 cyberattacks in sports history.

item.title: What Is an RFP Response? A Guide for Security and GRC Teams | UpGuard
item.description: Stop letting the RFP security section bottleneck your sales. Learn how GRC teams can streamline response workflows, map controls, and close deals faster

item.title: Implementing A Vendor Risk Assessment Process in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: A framework for implementing an efficient vendor risk assessment workflow into a Vendor Risk Management program in 2026.

item.title: Vendor Due Diligence Questionnaire (Free Template) | UpGuard
item.description: Learn all about vendor due diligence questionnaires, and customize one for your organization using our free VDDQ template.

item.title: Vendor Risk Assessments: An Ultimate Guide | UpGuard
item.description: Master vendor risk assessment: frameworks, real examples, and tools. Learn how automation and continuous monitoring strengthen your security.

item.title: How to Answer a Security Questionnaire: A 4-Step Guide | UpGuard
item.description: Learn best practices on how to answer a security questionnaire as a vendor.

item.title: What is a Security Questionnaire? | UpGuard
item.description: A security questionnaire helps organizations identify potential cybersecurity weaknesses among their third-party and even fourth-party vendors. Learn more.

item.title: Vendor Risk Management Checklist (Updated 2026) | UpGuard
item.description: This is a complete vendor risk management checklist. Learn how to reduce your third and fourth-party risk with this in-depth post.

item.title: NIST Enrichment Reductions Impact CVE Coverage, Accuracy
item.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) scaled back the number of CVEs it selects for in-depth analysis, but the move has produced mixed results, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: DOJ Seizes 400 Illegal FIFA World Cup Streaming Domains
item.description: <p>Officials from the US Department of Justice seized nearly 400 domains linked to illegal World Cup streams and warned viewers about the risks of malware, phishing, and data theft.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-doj-illegal-world-cup-streaming-domains/">DOJ Seizes 400 Illegal FIFA World Cup Streaming Domains</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Japanese Telecom Giant Says Breach May Expose 14.2 Million Email Accounts
item.description: <p>KDDI says a breach may have exposed email addresses and passwords for up to 14.2 million ISP accounts across six providers.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-kddi-breach-isp-email-accounts-apac-japan/">Japanese Telecom Giant Says Breach May Expose 14.2 Million Email Accounts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
item.description: The infostealer was delivered via CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp, targeting credentials linking development and admin environments to wider enterprise systems.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Vulnerabilities Expose Private Data in Indian Government Systems
item.description: One critical vulnerability, among many discovered by a researcher, could have allowed anyone to walk in and take over a national government portal.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Can Clothes Make You Invisible to Facial Recognition?
item.description: Does life feel Orwellian sometimes? One researcher has a solution for you: graphic tees that confuse the neural networks in surveillance cameras.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Iran, Russia, China Target Water Systems for Sabotage
item.description: Nation-state attackers breach water systems through weak passwords, exposed PLCs, and poor segmentation — not sophisticated malware.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: AI-Driven Identity Attacks Are Surging, PwC Warns
item.description: <p>AI has given cybercriminals a big advantage in attacking organizations, which they are using to go after weaknesses on edge devices</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-pwc-ai-identity-edge-device-cyber-threats/">AI-Driven Identity Attacks Are Surging, PwC Warns</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Drew Robb

item.title: WhatsApp is Finally Getting Usernames to Help Keep Phone Numbers Private
item.description:
WhatsApp on Monday officially announced the start of global reservations of usernames with an aim to protect the privacy of more than three billion users on the messaging platform.
The optional feature is designed to help users connect with someone on the service through usernames, as opposed to directly sharing their phone numbers. Username reservations will start rolling out starting today,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: North Korea-Linked macOS Malware Uses Prompt Injection to Evade AI Analysis
item.description: <p>SentinelOne says macOS.Gaslight uses prompt injection to mislead AI-based malware analysis, steal data, and use Telegram for C2.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-macos-gaslight-malware-ai-prompt-injection/">North Korea-Linked macOS Malware Uses Prompt Injection to Evade AI Analysis</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Factoring RSA Keys with Many Zeros
item.description: <p>Interesting research on a <a href="https://blog.trailofbits.com/2026/06/12/factoring-short-sleeve-rsa-keys-with-polynomials/">new class</a> of weak RSA keys: keys with lots of zeros. It turns out that these keys are out in the wild.</p> <blockquote><p>The badkeys project is an open-source service that checks public keys for known vulnerabilities. While developing this tool, Hanno collected a massive number of real-world keys from public sources, including Certificate Transparency logs, internet-wide TLS and SSH scans, PGP keys, and many others. By searching this dataset for unexpectedly sparse RSA moduli, we uncovered a large number of keys in the wild with the patterns in Figure 1...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Malicious Perplexity Chrome Extension Intercepted Searches and Address Bar Input
item.description:
Microsoft has found a malicious Chrome extension that posed as the AI search engine Perplexity and quietly logged what people searched for. It routed every query and every character typed into the address bar through an attacker-controlled server before redirecting users to real results.
Microsoft says Google removed it from the store after responsible disclosure. The extension was called "
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Apple Patches 30+ iOS, macOS, Safari Flaws, Including AI-Discovered WebKit Bugs
item.description:
Apple on Monday released security updates for iOS, macOS, and the Safari web browser to address over three dozen flaws, including four vulnerabilities in WebKit that were discovered using artificial intelligence (AI) tools like Anthropic Claude and OpenAI Codex Security.
The WebKit vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2026-43707 - A memory corruption issue that could result in an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Mustang Panda Uses Zoho WorkDrive as Command Channel in Indian Government Attacks
item.description:
The China-aligned espionage group&nbsp;Mustang Panda&nbsp;is running two campaigns against the Indian government and hydropower targets, deploying new malware and turning a legitimate cloud service into its command channel.
Acronis Threat Research Unit&nbsp;found active compromises inside Indian government networks, including machines used by senior administrative staff, and worked with&nbsp;
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Reveals GPT-5.6 Sol Cybersecurity Model, Restricts Early Access
item.description: OpenAI is previewing its GPT-5.6 Sol model to a vetted few at the US government's request

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Kernel Flaws, AI Malware Tricks, Turla Backdoor, Infostealers and More
item.description:
This week was a reminder that attackers do not always need big tricks. One small mistake, one old access path, one missed patch, and suddenly the door is open.
The noise is not all noise, either. Forums are talking, researchers are finding easy cracks, and defenders have more cleanup waiting.
Here’s the full Monday recap.
⚡ Threat of the Week
New DirtyClone Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Local
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Telegram-Based Millenium RAT Campaign Infects 60,000 Devices
item.description: Group-IB says Millenium RAT, now rewritten in C++, has hit 62,289 devices in 160+ countries

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Utimaco
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Update Chrome Now: Google Fixes 18 Security Flaws, Including Critical Bugs
item.description: <p>Google’s Chrome 149 security update fixes 18 bugs, including four critical flaws affecting WebGL, Autofill, and Blink components.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-chrome-149-security-update/">Update Chrome Now: Google Fixes 18 Security Flaws, Including Critical Bugs</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: 236,000 DCloud Uni-App Sites Used in Crypto Scams, Phishing, and Wallet Drainers
item.description:
New findings unearthed by Infoblox show that more than 236,000 websites are using investment scam templates built using a legitimate Chinese open-source, cross-platform application development framework called DCloud Uni-App.
The templates power bogus cryptocurrency exchanges, multi-language pig-butchering operations, WhatsApp phishing networks, fake gambling platforms, brand-impersonation
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Amazon Q VS Extension Flaw Leads to Cloud Credential Theft
item.description: Adversaries could plant a malicious repository that can execute arbitrary code and steal cloud credentials by exploiting the vulnerability, which showcases growing MCP risk.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Why Post-Quantum Cryptography Starts With Credentials
item.description: Today’s encrypted data, such as credentials, may no longer remain confidential in the future because the public-key cryptography protecting it will soon be broken by quantum computers. Although no machine today can break elliptic curve cryptography or RSA, quantum hardware is advancing rapidly and will inevitably change how organizations protect their data. Ciphertext and credentials captured by
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Gamaredon Expands Ukraine Attacks with New Malware and Cloud Service Abuse
item.description:
A Russian advanced persistent threat (APT) group has continued to evolve and expand its malware arsenal as part of its ongoing cyber onslaught against Ukraine throughout 2025.
Slovakian cybersecurity company ESET said it observed 35 distinct spear-phishing campaigns mounted by Gamaredon against new targets, with most of them taking place in the second half of the year. Primary targets of these
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Robot Police Officers
item.description: <p>We&#8217;ve taken one small step towards robot police officers: a drone capable of disarming a suspect:</p> <blockquote><p>In a <a href="https://www.instagram.com/reel/DZ4-tdPtbey/">June 22 video</a> posted on the Sacramento County Sheriff’s Office’s Instagram page, an officer wearing goggles can be seen operating a drone to retrieve a knife from an armed suspect hiding inside a cluttered house. &#8220;After not responding to negotiators, a drone was deployed inside the residence,&#8221; the post says. &#8220;Drone pilots located the suspect hiding in a corner of a garage&#8221; and then used a high-powered magnet attached to the drone to grab the knife out of the suspect&#8217;s hand. In the video ­ which is soundtracked by the &#8220;Mission: Impossible&#8221; theme song&#8212;the intercepted knife can be seen spinning around in the air as the drone carries it back to the deputies...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: US Federal Insurance Regulator Confirms Data Breach Via Oracle Flaw
item.description: An attacker has exploited a zero day in Oracle Peoplesoft to gain access to the IT systems of the NAIC, the standard-setting association for the US federal insurance system

item.title: Russian Hackers Accused of Destructive Cyber-Attack on Jaguar Land Rover
item.description: Experts warn the Jaguar Land Rover breach bears hallmarks of Kremlin-backed hackers, citing novel ransomware, strategic timing and efforts to obscure attribution

item.title: Inside the inbox: Why cybercriminals want to break into your email account
item.description: Your inbox is an identity system all of its own: whoever owns it may own a lot more

item.title: Microsoft Removes 119 Edge Extensions That Hid Malware in Images and Fonts
item.description:
Microsoft has shut down a long-running malicious extension operation on the Edge Add-ons store that hid its payloads inside ordinary image and font files, then woke up days after install to steal credentials and run ad fraud.
The company calls it&nbsp;StegoAd, a mash-up of steganography and adware, and ties 119 extensions to a single threat actor it says has been active since at least 2021.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: FBI Sounds Alarm Over Russian Intelligence Signal Phishing
item.description: The FBI claims Russian spies are targeting Signal backup keys

item.title: Public PoC Released for Critical libssh2 CVE-2026-55200 Client-Side SSH Flaw
item.description:
A public proof-of-concept is now out for CVE-2026-55200, a critical flaw in libssh2 that lets a malicious or compromised SSH server trigger memory corruption on a connecting client, with possible code execution. No credentials, no user interaction. The bug affects every release up to and including 1.11.1 and carries a CVSS 4.0 score of 9.2.
libssh2 is a client-side SSH library, not a server.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hijacked npm and Go Packages Use VS Code Tasks to Deploy Python Infostealer
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered two hijacked npm packages and a cluster of Go packages that are designed to deploy a Python-based information stealer on compromised Windows, Linux, and macOS hosts.
"This attack avoids the most common npm execution paths through lifecycle scripts, perhaps in an attempt to remain 'compatible' with npm v12's security hardenings," JFrog said in a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials
item.description:
The Security Service of Ukraine (SSU) said it, together with the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), uncovered a long-running campaign orchestrated by Russian intelligence services to break into the messaging accounts of government officials, military personnel, politicians, and activists in Ukraine, Europe, and the U.S.
The systematic cyber attacks aimed at stealing sensitive
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Previews GPT-5.6 Sol With Restricted Access and Stronger Cyber Safeguards
item.description:
OpenAI on Friday released three versions of GPT-5.6, called Sol, Terra, and Luna, as a limited preview to a small number of companies as part of an ongoing engagement with the U.S. government.
While Sol is the latest flagship model and the most powerful, Terra strikes a balance between efficiency and power, and Luna is fine-tuned for speed and affordability.
"GPT‑5.6 Sol launches with our most
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Third-Party Breaches Teach Education Sector a Costly Lesson in Vendor Risk
item.description: Rising threats from third-party actors are forcing institutions to play defense to protect student data from ransomware and other attacks.
item.dc:creator: Bree Fowler

item.title: The Chinese Control the Majority of Argentina’s Squid Fleet
item.description: <p>Chinese companies control nearly <a href="https://www.seafoodsource.com/news/environment-sustainability/chinese-companies-control-nearly-two-thirds-of-argentina-s-own-squid-fleet-according-to-new-iuu-expos">two-thirds</a> of Argentina’s own squid fleet.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup Recovery Keys
item.description:
The FBI and CISA have updated&nbsp;their March warning&nbsp;about Russian intelligence phishing Signal accounts, and the operators have added a step: they now coax targets into handing over their Signal Backup Recovery Key.
Hand it over once, and the attacker can restore the account's backup, read the private and group message history, and take over the account. Worse, the key keeps working.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Decline? Confidence in Autonomous Penetration Testing Falls
item.description: Companies are still experimenting with automated AI systems to find security weaknesses, but fewer are relying on the technology.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: New SharkLoader Malware Deploys Cobalt Strike in StrikeShark Cyberattacks
item.description:
A newly discovered cyber attack campaign has been observed delivering a previously undocumented malware family called SharkLoader that acts as a loader for deploying Cobalt Strike Beacon on compromised hosts.
Kaspersky, which is tracking the activity under the moniker StrikeShark, said the campaign has targeted a diplomatic organization in Indonesia, government organizations in Taiwan,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
item.description: Cisco joins a growing list of security platform providers that are betting that securing the agentic workforce means turning identity into the primary control plane.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Schwartz

item.title: Hackers Claim French Employment Leak Exposes Over 1M Records, Health Data
item.description: <p>Hackers claim 1M+ records tied to French employment apps were exposed, including HR files, health data, worker details, and plaintext passwords.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-akaolife-data-claim-emea-france/">Hackers Claim French Employment Leak Exposes Over 1M Records, Health Data</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: New License Plate Reader Tech Could Track Phones, AirPods, and Smartwatches
item.description: <p>Leonardo’s SignalTrace adds wireless device detection to ALPR systems, raising new questions about roadside surveillance, privacy, and security.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-leonardo-signaltrace-alpr-device-tracking/">New License Plate Reader Tech Could Track Phones, AirPods, and Smartwatches</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Meta Is Testing Facial Recognition for Police and Military
item.description: <p>We know that ICE wants to <a href="https://futurism.com/artificial-intelligence/ice-facial-surveillance-glasses">deploy</a> eyeglasses with facial recognition that can identify people in real time.</p> <p>Turns out Meta is <a href="https://www.wired.com/story/meta-rank-one-computing-face-recognition-smart-glasses/">prototyping</a> the feature with a Pentagon supplier. (Alternate <a href="https://gizmodo.com/meta-is-testing-police-surveillance-tech-for-its-smart-glasses-2000771931">news</a> story.)</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: New Initiative Tackles Security for End-of-Life Open Source Software
item.description: The Open Source Sustainability Initiative's goal is to help enterprises manage and secure aging open source projects while maintaining regulatory compliance.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Chinese-Speaking APT Deploys New TinyRCT Backdoor in Southeast Asia Campaign
item.description:
A Chinese-speaking advanced persistent threat (APT) actor has been linked to a new custom backdoor called TinyRCT as part of cyber attacks aimed at government entities and critical infrastructure in Southeast Asia.
The activity, particularly aimed at state-owned enterprises in the energy and government sectors, has been attributed to a threat actor called CL-STA-1062, which Palo Alto Networks
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Won't Wipe-Out Entry-Level Cybersecurity Jobs
item.description: Instead of eliminating jobs for early-career cyber pros, AI is creating new opportunities for candidates with strong human decision-making skills.
item.dc:creator: Jon France

item.title: Chinese AI Models Challenge OpenAI and Anthropic on Cost and Enterprise Risk
item.description: <p>Chinese AI models are undercutting OpenAI and Anthropic on price, but enterprise adoption depends on security, compliance, and data-governance trade-offs.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-chinese-ai-models-cost-risk/">Chinese AI Models Challenge OpenAI and Anthropic on Cost and Enterprise Risk</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: AI Upgrades, Security Breaches, and Industry Shakeups Define the Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from June 22–26.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-upgrades-security-breaches-and-industry-shakeups-define-the-week-in-tech-2/">AI Upgrades, Security Breaches, and Industry Shakeups Define the Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: New Linux pedit COW Exploit Enables Root Access by Poisoning Cached Binaries
item.description:
A flaw in the Linux kernel's traffic-control subsystem can let a local unprivileged user gain root on affected systems.
CVE-2026-46331, nicknamed "pedit COW," is an out-of-bounds write in the packet-editing action (act_pedit) that corrupts shared page-cache memory. A&nbsp;public, working exploit&nbsp;appeared within a day of the CVE assignment on June 16. Red Hat&nbsp;rates the flaw as
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Amazon Q Developer Flaw Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code via MCP Configs
item.description:
A high-severity flaw in Amazon Q Developer let a malicious repository run commands and steal a developer's cloud credentials. The path was short: a developer opens the repo, trusts the workspace, and Amazon Q does the rest. Amazon has patched it.
Tracked as&nbsp;CVE-2026-12957&nbsp;(CVSS 8.5), the bug sat in how Amazon's AI coding assistant handled Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers.
Wiz
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Meeting Trump's 2030 Quantum Deadline Will be Expensive, Complex
item.description: Getting accurate visibility into IT and OT systems will be compounded by multivendor environments, misaligned update life cycles, and interoperability gaps.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Thanks for Crushing the Submissions Inbox. We're Trying to Keep Up
item.description: It might be taking a bit longer than usual to respond to your submissions — here's why.
item.dc:creator: Becky Bracken

item.title: CISA Adds Exploited PTC Windchill RCE Flaw to KEV as Web Shell Attacks Continue
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a critical remote code execution vulnerability impacting PTC Windchill PDMlink and PTC FlexPLM enterprise Product Data Management (PDM) and Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) software to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation.
The vulnerability in question is
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
